馬

The year turns

Year of the Fire Horse

2026. Once in sixty years the Horse catches fire, a year that rewards the bold and scorches the hesitant. If the Horse is yours, this is your decade's hinge.

Read the Fire Horse

0 of 15 chapters

♌ Leo and 兔 Year of the Rabbit

Golden Pheasant

You carry a brilliance the forest cannot hide, even when you choose to stay unseen.

Zodi Animal · No. 052 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Golden Pheasant: Leo's radiance crossed with the Rabbit's watchful grace.

  • Leo

    gives you a brilliance that was built to be noticed.

  • The Rabbit

    gives you the perception to know exactly when attention is safe.

  • The Golden Pheasant

    turns both into one instinct: carry the display, and spend it only where it will land.

One strength, one cost: the same perception that keeps your brilliance safe can keep it permanently unseen. This reading traces where that instinct comes from, and what it becomes when it grows up.

Meet the Golden Pheasant

The most spectacular bird in the forest, and the hardest one to see

A bird of central China's mountain forests, wearing gold, crimson and deep blue where nothing can see it.

Everything this reading says about you is grounded in what the Golden Pheasant actually is: a spectacular display carried through dense undergrowth, a cape that opens only when the bird decides, a body that would rather run than fly. This chapter is that foundation. The rest of the page draws meaning from these real traits.

The colors were never the problem. The clearing is.

6 traits below

01of six

The cape

Animal fact

Behind the head sits a ruff of orange, black-edged feathers. In courtship the male raises it and rotates it forward until it screens his face, fanning the tail and circling the hen. The cape stays folded the rest of the time.

Our reading

Your display is deliberate, not automatic. When it opens, everyone present understands they are being shown something you decided to show.

02of six

Hidden brilliance

Animal fact

The species is endemic to the forested mountains of central China, and despite the plumage it is secretive to the point of near-invisibility, keeping to dense undergrowth where it is rarely seen even where it is common.

Our reading

Being conspicuous and being seen are two different things. You have solved for the first and quietly declined the second.

03of six

The tail

Animal fact

The adult male runs 90 to 105 centimetres long, and roughly two thirds of that is tail. Most of what the bird carries is not the part that eats, fights, or flies.

Our reading

A great deal of what you carry has no practical function. It is beauty, and carrying it costs you, and you carry it anyway.

04of six

The plain hen

Animal fact

The female wears mottled brown, cryptic against leaf litter. One species, two answers to the same forest: one bird solved it by vanishing, the other by dazzling and then hiding.

Our reading

Both instincts live in you. The brilliance and the camouflage are the same animal, and neither one is a mask over the other.

05of six

Runs, rarely flies

Animal fact

Golden pheasants are clumsy fliers that run from a disturbance rather than take off, feeding on the ground through the day.

Our reading

Your first answer to threat is distance, taken quietly, on foot. You leave a situation before most people notice it turned.

06of six

Roosts high

Animal fact

The bird forages on the forest floor by day and climbs into the canopy to roost at night, sleeping above everything that walks.

Our reading

You need a place with height and no traffic to end the day in. Without it, the watching never switches off.

Two zodiacs, one animal

Where Leo and the Rabbit meet

Leo brings

  • Real brilliance, and the appetite to be seen for it
  • Warmth that changes the temperature the moment you arrive
  • A standard for how the moment ought to look

The Rabbit brings

  • Perception fine enough to read intent before it is spoken
  • A preference for the edge over the center
  • An accurate, unsentimental sense of danger

What the crossing makes

Cross them and you get a creature built for the center of the clearing that spends its life at the treeline. The brilliance is real. The reluctance is real. Neither is performance, and each keeps the other honest: the Rabbit prevents Leo from spending itself on company that was never worth it, and Leo prevents the Rabbit from calling the treeline a life.

Where they pull against each other

Leo needs to be seen; the Rabbit needs to be safe. One wants the clearing, the other has already counted the exits. Fused well, that is taste: you spend your brilliance where it lands. Fused badly, the perception becomes a condition-generator, and the conditions never all arrive at once. The chapters ahead are largely about telling discernment from delay.

Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

Leo
Rabbit
50 Leo In balance Rabbit 50

In balance, you scan the clearing, decide it is good enough, and walk in. You spend your brilliance deliberately, on the ones you chose, and you do not require them to be perfect first.

Your element

木

Your element is Wood

Wood's archetype: The Pioneer · in the Golden Pheasant: The Patient Kindling

Wood's archetype in this system is the Pioneer. In the Golden Pheasant it slows into something more specific: the Patient Kindling, the fuel that keeps deciding the fire it feeds is not ready to be lit. Wood is growth with a direction: benevolence, vision, flexible strength, the upward push of spring into form. In excess it grows crooked around an obstacle rather than through it: hesitation dressed as timing, resentment held quietly, a nature that bends so well it never has to declare itself. The Shadow chapter ahead wears this same signature.

How this was calculated

Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal, and for the Rabbit that is Wood. Your Western sign adds a second note: Leo carries Fire, and in the five-phase cycle Wood feeds Fire. So the two halves are not rivals. The Rabbit's Wood is the fuel; Leo's Fire is what that fuel is for. Your element is the wood in the hand of an animal still deciding whether tonight is the night to light it. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

DirectionEast
SeasonSpring
VirtueBenevolence
ColorGreen

The elements around you

Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

Generates you

水Water nourishes you

Water feeds Wood the way rain feeds a root you cannot see. Deep, unhurried, perceptive people restore you; noise does not. What looks like withdrawal is often you going back to the water.

Balances you

金Metal cuts you to shape

Metal prunes Wood, and pruning is not injury. Clear-edged people and hard deadlines are what turn your endless growing of conditions back into a shape that can be seen.

You generate

火You generate Fire

Wood feeds Fire, and the Fire it feeds here is your own Leo half. The brilliance is not borrowed from anywhere. You are carrying its fuel, which is why the display costs you and why nobody else can light it.

You break open

土You open Earth

Roots split packed ground. Your perception does the same to a settled situation: quietly, from underneath, until what looked solid has to admit what it was sitting on.

Go deeper: Bazi 八字

This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

Your Wood comes from one place: the Rabbit, whose fixed element anchors the whole Golden Pheasant. But Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water are not fixed labels. They are a grammar for how energy feeds and wears on itself, and the Rabbit's Wood is only your starting note.

Bazi 八字, the Four Pillars, reads that grammar across your whole chart: year, month, day, and the exact hour you were born. The Golden Pheasant takes the element of just one pillar, your Rabbit year. Your full chart also carries the Fire that Leo lends, in proportions only your birth time can show.

BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

Go deeper: Chakras

Wood in the body

Held beside the yogic map, Wood finds its closest match in the Heart chakra, Anahata: the opening at the center of the chest, where warmth is either given or held. Its bija sound is YAM; its element there is air, and its guardian is a black antelope, an animal that runs first and is seen second.

Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

Heart chakraAnahataBija: YAMElement: air
ChakraVisit your Heart chakra PracticeYoga for your element

Take it further

木ElementThe Wood element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

The Golden Pheasant dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 052 / 144

☼ Golden Pheasant · Leo × Rabbit

The Hidden Sovereign

Built for the clearing, fluent in the undergrowth, and careful with the light.

Profile

Archetype
The Hidden Sovereign
Central gift
Reading a room to its floor
Central shadow
Waiting for a safe clearing
Protective instinct
Watch first, enter later
Growth lesson
Step out before it is perfect
Power phase
New Moon
Element
Wood
Ideal habitat
Deep cover, one clear path into light

These five traits are yours too. Take the test to see whether you hold back the way the Golden Pheasant holds back, or whether you are a rare one who carries the colors openly.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to read it

    Golden PheasantPick a trait above to see how the Golden Pheasant wears it, and which side it leans toward.

    The Primal Mirror

    How this lives inside you

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    01 · Reflex

    Instinct: your first move

    You find the cover, you read the gathering, and you hold the display folded.

    Before thought gets a vote, you have located the edge of the space: the late seat, the wall, the position with a view of the door. Watching is not what you do while waiting to participate. It is the participation.

    The brilliance does not go anywhere while you watch. It stays folded, warm, entirely intact, waiting on a judgment you have not consciously made yet.

    02 · Capacity

    Gift: what you do better than most

    You read a room to its floor, and when you do open, it is real.

    That same reflex, given air, becomes two rare things at once. You perceive a situation to a depth most people never reach, which makes you an extraordinary counsel to the few you let close. And because you never open the cape casually, opening it means something. People do not forget the day you were fully present.

    Nothing you show has been rehearsed for approval. That is the capacity. Hold that thought; it returns in the next two states.

    03 · Defense

    Guard: what you protect, and how

    You keep the beautiful thing out of the reach of people who would handle it badly.

    What the gift is guarding is the display itself: your taste, your warmth, the unarmored center of you. You protect it by staying in cover and running early, and you extend the same protection to the people you love, standing at the treeline reading the weather for them.

    Somewhere in your history, being visible cost you something, and you were watching closely enough to learn the exact lesson. It is real devotion. Defenses this accurate do not know when to stand down.

    04 · Distortion

    Shadow: the guard, overgrown

    Perception this fine can always find one more reason the clearing is not ready.

    What trips it. Being seen before you chose it, or standing under a light that demands the display before you have finished reading it.

    What your mind says. This is not the place. I will show them when it is.

    What you do. You add a condition. Then another. The cape stays folded for one more season, and you call it standards.

    What it costs. The people who would have loved what you carry never find out it exists, so they stop looking, and you read their looking away as proof you were right to hide. <b>A color that never leaves the undergrowth is not protected. It is buried.</b>

    In love

    You give a partner the reading and withhold the warmth, then wonder why they feel studied instead of loved.

    At work

    You knew the answer in the first ten minutes and said it in the corridor afterward, to one person.

    With friends

    You are the one everyone confides in and nobody knows, and you have let that arrangement stand for years.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    You still read the gathering. You walk into it anyway, and the reading comes with you.

    The awakened Golden Pheasant still finds the cover, still reads the gathering to its floor, still holds the display for something worth it. What changes is the threshold: good enough replaces perfect, because perfect was never an arrival, it was a way of not going. You spend the perception on choosing which clearing, not on proving that none of them qualify.

    You step out before the conditions are right, and the light is ordinary, and the people there turn out to have been waiting.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I am still safe when I

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best where judgment is the product, where your read is asked for directly, and where nobody makes you perform it in front of a crowd.

    01 / 05

    You lead by being right in private

    You lead through accuracy rather than volume. You see the flaw in the plan on the first pass, and the people who have learned to ask you get an answer worth more than the meeting that produced the plan.

    The failure mode is structural. Influence that only ever travels through side conversations never becomes authority, and the room slowly forgets to attribute the good call to you.

    02 / 05

    Cover, warning, and a real audience

    Advance notice before you are asked to speak. Work that is judged on its quality rather than on the confidence of its delivery. A small number of colleagues who have earned the whole of you.

    Environments that drain this animal: forced improvisation, open-plan rooms with no edge to stand at, cultures where visibility is the currency and the loudest read wins.

    • Advance notice
    • Craft over performance
    • Small trusted circle
    • An edge to stand at

    03 / 05

    Slow to commit, reluctant to be visible

    Your decision pattern is the Rabbit's: gather until the picture is complete, then move once, cleanly. That serves you when the stakes are high and punishes you when the window is short. Set a deadline for the reading, not for the decision, and let the deadline be earlier than feels safe.

    Your delegation pattern is Leo's inverted: you hand out the work and keep the credit invisible. Practice the reverse once a quarter. Present the thing yourself, badly if necessary, and let the audience attach your face to your judgment.

    04 / 05

    Two ways the display stays folded

    The first: you are asked to show it before you are ready, and the whole animal goes still. You give a competent, careful, weightless answer while the real one sits behind your teeth.

    The second is quieter. You defer the display so long that deferral becomes the identity, and then it is not fear anymore, it is taste, and taste is much harder to argue with. The burnout pattern of this profile is not collapse. It is a slow, articulate resignation, delivered by someone who was never once wrong.

    05 / 05

    Reputation, built one clearing at a time

    Your edge is judgment, and judgment compounds only when it is attributed. The long game is building a body of visible calls, made in your own name, in gatherings that were merely good enough.

    One growth practice: each quarter, say the accurate thing in the meeting instead of in the corridor. Track what it cost you. The number will be smaller than the animal predicted.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    Deep cover, with one clear path into light.

    The golden pheasant feeds on the shaded floor and climbs to roost above everything at night. Its human translation: enclosure you control, height you can retreat to, and exactly one place in the home where you are fully lit. Every recommendation below follows from that: green depth because Wood is your element, one bright threshold because the clearing has to exist somewhere, and never your back to a space you have not already read.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Wood is your growth; Water is what feeds it. The bar shows roughly how much of each to use, and every color carries a light and a dark tint, so you can build a shaded forest floor or a room that opens.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundWoodAbout 45% of the room

    Qinling forest green

    Your own Wood tone, deep and shaded. The cover the room rests in: walls, floors, the large quiet surfaces.

    SupportWoodAbout 29% of the room

    New shoot green

    Wood's living tone, the growth that has not hardened. Textiles, plants, mid-size pieces, anything the light touches daily.

    AccentWaterAbout 17% of the room

    Wing-bar teal

    Water feeds Wood, so this teal is what keeps your growth from going brittle. It is also the blue the bird actually wears. Cushions, art, smaller pieces.

    SparkWaterAbout 9% of the room

    Undergrowth indigo

    Concentrated Water, the deepest note in the room. One or two small touches, so the depth reads as rest rather than as hiding.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    A desk with your back to a wall and clear sight of the door. This animal cannot concentrate while any part of it is still watching the entrance.

    Bedroom

    The highest, quietest room available, kept dim and green. The pheasant roosts above the traffic, and so does the part of you that finally stops reading.

    Entry

    One threshold that is genuinely bright, with a mirror you pass rather than face. The house should have one place where being seen is easy and brief.

    Living area

    An edge seat with a full view, and one warm lamp that turns that seat into the center when you decide it should be.

    Display shelf

    One shelf, lit, holding one thing you made rather than one thing you bought. Visible from where guests sit. You do not have to mention it.

    Reflection

    A shaded corner with something green and living in it, for the new-moon practice of naming the clearing you will walk into next.

    Elements to avoid

    • A seat with its back to the room; the watching never stops there
    • Rooms with no edge and no exit; cover is not optional for this animal
    • Hard, bright, unbroken light in every room; Wood grows in shade and reaches for one opening
    • Storing the beautiful things you own where nobody will ever see them
    The five-minute reset

    Five-minute habitat reset: move one thing you made into the lit part of the room, water the living green thing, and sit in the seat that faces the door until the reading finishes and the room goes quiet.

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Wood element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the clearing

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each part of your life.

    • The lesson

      Step out before it is perfect. Perfect was never a room. It was the condition that kept you waiting.

    • A ritual

      At each new moon, name out loud the one clearing you will walk into this cycle, and the condition you are dropping in order to do it.

    • In your space

      Keep exactly one thing you made where guests can see it, lit, unexplained. Rotate it when the next thing is finished.

    • In one bond

      This week, tell one person something about yourself that they did not have to ask for. Small is fine. First is the point.

    • At work

      Say the accurate thing in the meeting rather than in the corridor afterward. Once. Then write down what it actually cost.

    Keeper Stones

    Four stones keep this animal

    Each one answers a different Golden Pheasant tension. Pick one up; the one you choose stays on your card.

    Anchor stone

    Labradorite

    Why it belongs here

    Grey and unremarkable until the light hits it at one angle, and then it is blue and gold fire. It belongs to the Golden Pheasant because it holds the animal's whole problem in one object.

    What it supports

    Trusting that the display is still there while it is folded, the anxiety the signature Perception axis feeds on.

    A simple use

    Keep it face down on the desk. Turn it over on the days you are about to talk yourself out of being seen.

    Care

    Softer than quartz; keep it from knocks and grit.

    Clarity stone

    Nephrite jade

    Why it belongs here

    Green, tough, and quiet: Wood in mineral form, and the stone the Rabbit has been carved from for three thousand years.

    What it supports

    Telling discernment from delay, the one judgment this animal most needs to make and least wants to.

    A simple use

    Hold it while you name the condition you are about to add, and ask out loud whether it is taste or fear.

    Care

    Very tough; warm water and a soft cloth.

    Courage stone

    Sunstone

    Why it belongs here

    A stone that flashes copper when it turns, the Leo half of the crossing made small enough to carry.

    What it supports

    Walking into a clearing that is good enough, on the days the Rabbit's caution wins an argument it should lose.

    A simple use

    Carry it on days that require entering, not observing.

    Care

    Durable; avoid harsh chemicals.

    Boundary stone

    Obsidian

    Why it belongs here

    Volcanic glass that shows you your own face, dark and exact. A boundary stone for an animal whose boundary is a treeline.

    What it supports

    Keeping cover chosen rather than automatic, the shadow's specific imbalance.

    A simple use

    Place it at the entry, where you decide each day whether you are stepping out or staying in.

    Care

    Chips easily; keep it off hard surfaces.

    Stones are cultural and symbolic tools, not medical treatment. Some fade in sunlight or dislike water; follow each care note.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The new moon is the month's cover: no light, nothing on display, the whole cycle folded shut. It fits the Golden Pheasant because this is the one animal that needs darkness to be a decision rather than a habit. What to do: in the dark, where nobody is watching, name the clearing you will walk into this cycle. What to avoid: enjoying the dark so much that naming it is all you do.

    Your next full moon · Power Moon

    Tuesday, July 14, 2026

    5 days from today.

    When it arrives, sit in the dark you have always found comfortable and name one clearing. The dark was never the problem for you. Leaving it on a night that is merely good enough is.

    1. Mon Jul 13The dark before. Write down every condition you have attached to being seen this year.
    2. Tue Jul 14 · New MoonThe turn. Name one clearing and one condition you are dropping in order to walk into it.
    3. Wed Jul 15The first light. Do the smallest visible version of it before the perception talks you out.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    守shǒu株zhū待dài兔tù

    Tap a character to hear it

    Literally guard the stump, wait for the rabbit

    Meaning A caution against waiting where effort is needed. Fortune that arrived once will not return to the same still hand.

    The farmer keeps his post because it worked once, and the field goes unplanted. You keep the treeline because it kept you safe once, and the clearing stays empty. The stump is not the problem. The waiting is what you have mistaken for a plan.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Rabbit. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Golden Pheasant loves

    You show love by seeing people precisely, and you rarely let anyone return the favor.

    Care, for you, is attention: the exact read, the anticipated need, the beautiful thing arranged before anyone arrives. The pattern underneath shapes every bond differently. Being known is the thing you want most and the thing you postpone hardest.

    How you show love

    By noticing. You know what a partner needs before they name it, and you build them a beautiful evening as a way of saying something you have not said out loud.

    What makes you feel safe

    Being looked at steadily and liked anyway. Attention that does not turn into an audience.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your watchfulness can read as distance or judgment. It is neither. It is how you stay in the room.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go quiet, run early, and process the whole argument alone at a distance where it cannot reach you. You return with a conclusion nobody watched you form.

    What to practice

    Say the unfinished thought out loud, while it is still unfinished, to the person it concerns.

    How you show up

    As the one who remembers, who asks the question nobody else thought to ask, and who arranges the table so the evening has a chance.

    What they may misunderstand

    Because you hold everyone's confidences and volunteer none of your own, friends assume you have nothing heavy to carry. Then you count who never asked.

    What to practice

    Tell one friend one thing about yourself that they did not draw out of you. Let it be small. Let it be first.

    How you show up

    You read the weather of the household before you cross the threshold, and you adjust everything about yourself to whatever you found.

    What they may misunderstand

    They think you are easy. You are composed, and you are the one doing the composing.

    What to practice

    Enter once without scanning first. Say hello as yourself and let the household adjust to you.

    How you show up

    The accurate one at the edge of the table, whose one comment reorganizes the discussion when it finally comes.

    Your conflict pattern

    You withdraw the display rather than defend it, and let a weaker idea win a room you had already read correctly.

    What to practice

    Say it in the meeting. The corridor is where good judgment goes to be forgotten.

    Healthy support, for this animal, is specific: people who go looking for you, gently and repeatedly, and who do not flinch when the whole display finally opens. Choose them over the ones who admire the cover story. Being seen accurately is not exposure. It is the only thing that was ever going to make the colors worth carrying.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    水

    Honey Badger

    ♈ Aries × 猪 Pig

    1947, 1959, 1971, 1983, 1995, 2007, 2019

    Same trineTheir Water feeds your Wood

    The Pig shares the Rabbit's trine, so effort between you compounds. Their Water feeds your Wood: this one is fuel.

    Read the Honey Badger

    Match II · of three

    木

    Fennec Fox

    ♊ Gemini × 兔 Rabbit

    1951, 1963, 1975, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2023

    Same yearShared Wood

    Two Rabbit years run on the same clock. Two Wood natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Fennec Fox

    Match III · of three

    土

    Sunbittern

    ♌ Leo × 狗 Dog

    1946, 1958, 1970, 1982, 1994, 2006, 2018

    Secret friendsEarth nature

    The Dog is the Rabbit's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair.

    Read the Sunbittern

    Walk further in

    Where to go from the Golden Pheasant

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Leo changes across the other eleven years

    You have watched Leo fuse with the Rabbit. The fastest way to feel the system's logic is to watch the same brilliance land in eleven different bodies, most of which have no interest in hiding it.

    All Leo animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    Watchers and hiders with something bright folded away: kinship, not similarity.

    Pallas's CatScorpio · Year of the Rabbit Penduline TitVirgo · Year of the Rabbit Blue-ringed OctopusLeo · Year of the Snake

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Display without hesitation, and boldness without a read: your pattern, inverted.

    Harpy EagleLeo · Year of the Tiger Paradise RiflebirdLeo · Year of the Horse

    Same year, other skies

    The Rabbit's perception under eleven other suns.

    SpringbokAries · Year of the Rabbit KoalaTaurus · Year of the Rabbit Fennec FoxGemini · Year of the Rabbit Sea OtterCancer · Year of the Rabbit

    Same sky, other years

    Leo's brilliance wearing eleven other bodies.

    Golden Lion TamarinLeo · Year of the Rat Spotted HyenaLeo · Year of the Ox Rhinoceros HornbillLeo · Year of the Dragon SunbitternLeo · Year of the Dog

    Step back and see the system

    The hubs this crossing hangs from.

    The last chapter

    The Golden Pheasant is yours.
    Now find out who is worth the clearing.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Golden Pheasant.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the new moon, your power phase, and name the clearing in the dark. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Hidden Sovereign · Leo × Rabbit · Wood 木

    Your line to keep

    I am the Golden Pheasant: built for the clearing, fluent in the undergrowth, and careful with the light.

    雉

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Golden Pheasant now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Love

    Who the Golden Pheasant is built to love

    You wait for someone who has earned the display. One animal already has.

    Not the one who admires the cover. The one who keeps looking until you open.

    See who matches     Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Golden Pheasant wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Some people are worth the clearing. Some are exactly why you stayed in the trees. The wheel tells you which.

    Run the Bond Test     Element 木

    Your Wood runs through eleven other animals

    The Golden Pheasant is one of twelve carrying Wood. The other eleven share your ground and bend it differently.

    Same element, twelve temperaments. A few will feel like family the moment you read them.

    See the Wood twelve

    Keep the thread before it closes.

    A free account saves this reading, your line, and the animals you test. Come back to the exact page you left, not a blank start.

    Takes about a minute. No card. You can delete it anytime.

    Opens the share sheet.

    Golden Pheasant · 052 / 144 · Wood 木

    From your Zodi Animal reading

    Send it out

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Golden Pheasant until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Golden Pheasant, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Golden Pheasant Test

    Do you hold the display the way the Golden Pheasant holds it?

    The Golden Pheasant is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Golden Pheasant, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Golden Pheasant You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here the Hidden Sovereign, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Chrysolophus pictus
      • eBird · Golden Pheasant
      • Zhang et al. · Cape feather coloration and MHC genotype in male golden pheasants

      Last reviewed 2026-07-09

      Your Readingabout 6 min Meet the AnimalThe CrossingYour ElementThe DossierThe Primal Mirror Your Lifeabout 5 min Working NatureIdeal HabitatGrowth Go Deeperbrowse Keeper StonesMoon RhythmYour Proverb Your Peopleplay RelationshipsRelated AnimalsThe Bond Test

      The Primal Zodiac, 144 animals

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      Find your Zodi Animal ✦

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.
